title: asclepias pedunculata
intro: Asclepias pedunculata, also known as the Butterfly Milkweed or Clasping Milkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the milkweed family (Asclepiadaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Asclepias pedunculata include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. Asclepias pedunculata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
